# Test Fixtures

These `.npz` files are the ground truth for all Python validation tests.
They are generated by running the original MATLAB NLvib examples and saving the outputs.

## How to generate

```bash
# 1. Download the original MATLAB source
bash tools/fetch_matlab_source.sh

# 2. Generate fixtures (requires MATLAB or Octave on PATH)
python tools/generate_fixtures.py

# 3. Or for a single example:
python tools/generate_fixtures.py --example 01_Duffing --engine octave
```

## Fixture format

Each `.npz` file contains:

| Array | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `Om_HB` / `Om` | Excitation frequency values (rad/s) along solution branch |
| `Q_HB` / `Q` | Displacement amplitude coefficients (n_dof × n_harmonics × n_points) |
| `Om_shoot` | Frequency values from shooting method (if present) |
| `Q_shoot` | Displacements from shooting method (if present) |

## Tolerance

All Python results are validated against these fixtures with:

```python
rel_error = |python - matlab| / (|matlab| + 1e-12)
assert rel_error.max() <= 1e-6
```

## MATLAB version

Generated with: _(fill in when generating)_
NLvib branch: NLvib-Basic (default) or NLvib-PEACE
